title: How to get a police certificate - Brunei - Canada.ca
content:
## How to get a police certificate - Brunei
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

## Name of the document you need
Police Certificate of Good Conduct

## Police certificate and IRCC requirements
You do not need to provide a police certificate with your application. We’ll send you a letter to ask for a police certificate after you send your application for
- some temporary residence programs
You must bring that letter when you apply for a police certificate.
See the document checklist and the visa office requirements for details .
Apply to the Commissioner of Police (see Contact information for complete address) after you get an official request letter.
To get a certificate you can
- apply through the Brunei Darussalam embassy or consulate where you live
- ask a representative in Brunei Darussalam to get a certificate on your behalf
Only apply after you get an official request letter.

## Information and documents you need
- Letter requesting a police certificate from us
- A copy of your passport work permit (if available) identity card (if available)
- work permit (if available)
- identity card (if available)
- Applicable fees To be paid in cash or by a bank draft payable to the Commissioner of the Royal Brunei Police Force
- To be paid in cash or by a bank draft payable to the Commissioner of the Royal Brunei Police Force
Royal Brunei Police Force Headquarters Tungku Gadong BE2110 Negara Brunei Darussalam Telephone: 673-2-423901 / 2459500 Fax: 673-2429040 / 2454394 Email: rbpf.pro@police.gov.bn Website: Royal Brunei Police Force Headquarters (available in Malay only)
